channel.title: dout.dev — Latest Posts
channel.description: Vanilla-first static blog with WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility

item.title: CSS Properties Hierarchy (Or: How I Learned Custom Properties Can Replace Preprocessors)
item.description: I've been writing CSS since before border-radius was a thing, back when we used -moz-border-radius and prayed everything to work well.

item.title: A Pragmatic Service Worker: Cache Strategy, Offline, No Abuse (80 Lines, No Drama)
item.description: The case against most service workers

item.title: Honest Lazy Loading: IntersectionObserver vs Native loading=lazy (The Real Difference)
item.description: The two tools, in one paragraph

item.title: Accessible Scrollspy and Outline Without contenteditable (Yes, It Can Be Done)
item.description: The feature and its trap

item.title: A Custom Element for Syntax Highlight: Why I Wrote `pix-highlighter`
item.description: The stack I did not want

item.title: Pi and DeepSeek V4 Flash: The Daily Coding Loop That Costs Almost Nothing
item.description: The headline numbers

item.title: Client-Side Search on a Static Dataset (No Server, No Algolia, No Bullshit)
item.description: The constraint (no backend, no excuses)

item.title: The End of Borrowed Abstractions
item.description: The reflex that shaped software

item.title: Why DisplayPreferencesPopover Exists (Accessibility Is Not a Checklist, It's User Preferences)
item.description: Accessibility is not one feature (it's ALL of them)

item.title: How PixHighlighter Is Built (Or: I Wrote a Syntax Highlighter Because Prism Was Too Bloaty)
item.description: The contract stays plain (because HTML should be readable)

item.title: WCAG 2.2 AA Without ARIA-Spam (Landmarks, Heading Order, Skip-Links — That's 80% of It)
item.description: The unpopular opinion (brace yourself)

item.title: Modern CSS Is Enough (Container Queries, Nesting, `:has()` — No Framework Required)
item.description: The short version (for the impatient)

item.title: Vibe Coding With Rigor (Or: How to Use AI Without Your Codebase Turning Into a Dumpster Fire)
item.description: The term I am reclaiming (because "vibe coding" got a bad rap)

item.title: CI/CD Around GitHub Pages (Or: How I Got Deploy Previews Without Paying Netlify)
item.description: The gap GitHub Pages leaves open (it's not that bad)

item.title: Quality Gate: Linting, Formatting, and Verify (Or: How I Stopped Shipping Broken HTML)
item.description: Why the gate exists (because you will ship shit if you don't)

item.title: SEO Metadata Without Rituals (Or: I Read 47 SEO Blog Posts So You Don't Have To)
item.description: The short version (TL;DR for the impatient)

item.title: OG Images at Build Time (Or: How I Stopped Worrying and Learned to Love Sharp)
item.description: The problem and the usual solutions (none of which I wanted)

item.title: Feeds and Sitemaps at Build Time (RSS Is Dead, Long Live RSS. Also JSON Feed.)
item.description: The distribution minimum (three files, that's it)

item.title: Build Assets Pipeline (Or: How I Turned a Simple Blog Into an Image Processing Factory)
item.description: The goal (four non-negotiable constraints)

item.title: Design Tokens Before Pages (Yes, I Wrote the CSS Before the First Line of HTML)
item.description: The mistake I kept making (repeat after me: tokens first)
